items.content:
Use claude-code for free in the terminal, VSCode extension or via discord like openclaw

Claude Code running via NVIDIA NIM, completely free

40 req/min free on NVIDIA NIM. Free models on OpenRouter. Fully local with LM Studio
Set 2 env vars. No modifications to Claude Code CLI or VSCode extension needed
NVIDIA NIM, OpenRouter, DeepSeek, LM Studio (local), llama.cpp ()
Route Opus / Sonnet / Haiku to different models and providers. Mix providers freely
Parses tags and into native Claude thinking blocks
Models outputting tool calls as text are auto-parsed into structured tool use
5 categories of trivial API calls intercepted locally, saving quota and latency
Proactive rolling-window throttle + reactive 429 exponential backoff + optional concurrency cap
Remote autonomous coding with tree-based threading, session persistence, and live progress
Task tool interception forces . No runaway subagents
Clean and ABCs. Add new providers or platforms easily
# Install uv (required to run the project)
pip install uv

If uv is already installed, run uv self update to get the latest version.

git clone https://github.com/Alishahryar1/free-claude-code.git
cd free-claude-code
cp .env.example .env

Choose your provider and edit :

Migration: was removed in this release. Rename it to .

Start the proxy server:

uv run uvicorn server:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8082

Run Claude Code:

Point at the proxy root URL, not .

$env:ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN="freecc"; $env:ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL="http://localhost:8082"; claude

ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN="freecc" ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL="http://localhost:8082" claude

That's it! Claude Code now uses your configured provider for free.

Install as a Package (no clone needed)

uv tool install git+https://github.com/Alishahryar1/free-claude-code.git
fcc-init        # creates ~/.config/free-claude-code/.env from the built-in template

Edit ~/.config/free-claude-code/.env with your API keys and model names, then:

free-claude-code    # starts the server

To update: uv tool upgrade free-claude-code

┌─────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────┐
│  Claude Code    │───────>│  Free Claude Code    │───────>│  LLM Provider    │
│  CLI / VSCode   │<───────│  Proxy (:8082)       │<───────│  NIM / OR / LMS  │
└─────────────────┘        └──────────────────────┘        └──────────────────┘
   Anthropic API                                             OpenAI-compatible
   format (SSE)                                             format (SSE)
  • : Claude Code sends standard Anthropic API requests; the proxy forwards them to your configured provider
  • : Opus / Sonnet / Haiku requests resolve to their model-specific backend, with as fallback
  • : 5 categories of trivial requests (quota probes, title generation, prefix detection, suggestions, filepath extraction) are intercepted and responded to locally without using API quota
  • : Requests are translated from Anthropic format to the provider's OpenAI-compatible format and streamed back
  • : tags and fields are converted into native Claude thinking blocks when

The proxy also exposes Claude-compatible probe routes: , , POST /v1/messages/count_tokens, plus / support for the common probe endpoints.

Daily driver, generous free tier
Model variety, fallback options
Direct access to DeepSeek chat/reasoner
Privacy, offline use, no rate limits
Lightweight local inference engine

Models use a prefix format: provider_prefix/model/name. An invalid prefix causes an error.

integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1

Control Claude Code remotely from Discord (or Telegram). Send tasks, watch live progress, and manage multiple concurrent sessions.

  • Tree-based message threading: reply to a message to fork the conversation
  • Session persistence across server restarts
  • Live streaming of thinking tokens, tool calls, and results
  • Unlimited concurrent Claude CLI sessions (concurrency controlled by )
  • Voice notes: send voice messages; they are transcribed and processed as regular prompts
  • Commands: (cancel a task; reply to a message to stop only that task), (reset all sessions, or reply to clear a branch),

MESSAGING_PLATFORM="discord"
DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN="your_discord_bot_token"
ALLOWED_DISCORD_CHANNELS="123456789,987654321"

Enable Developer Mode in Discord (Settings → Advanced), then right-click a channel and "Copy ID". Comma-separate multiple channels. If empty, no channels are allowed.

ALLOWED_DIR="C:/Users/yourname/projects"

uv run uvicorn server:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8082

Whisper/Parakeet models via gRPC

Adding an OpenAI-compatible provider (Groq, Together AI, etc.) — extend :

from providers.openai_compat import OpenAICompatibleProvider
from providers.base import ProviderConfig

class MyProvider(OpenAICompatibleProvider):
    def __init__(self, config: ProviderConfig):
        super().__init__(config, provider_name="MYPROVIDER",
                         base_url="https://api.example.com/v1", api_key=config.api_key)

Adding a fully custom provider — extend directly and implement .

Adding a messaging platform — extend in and implement , , , , and .

items.title: Anil-matcha/Open-Generative-AI
items.content:
Uncensored, open-source alternative to Higgsfield AI, Freepik AI, Krea AI, Openart AI — Free, unrestricted AI image & video generation studio with 200+ models (Flux, Midjourney, Kling, Sora, Veo). No content filters. Self-hosted, MIT licensed.

The free, open-source, unrestricted alternative to Higgsfield AI, Freepik, Krea, Openart AI. Generate AI images and videos using 200+ state-of-the-art models — no content filters, no closed ecosystem, no subscription fees.

🐎 Early access to Happy Horse 1.0 — Alibaba's #1 ranked AI video model. Check out Awesome HappyHorse 1.0 API & Prompts — a Python wrapper plus a curated library of high-performing community prompts for native 1080p text-to-video and image-to-video generation with jointly generated audio.

💡 Looking for GPT-Image-2 prompts? Check out Awesome GPT-Image-2 API Prompts — a curated collection of 40+ ready-to-use prompts for the OpenAI API covering portraits, posters, UI mockups, game screenshots, and more.

🤖 Automate Higgsfield, Freepik, Krea, Openart & more with AI coding agents:Generative-Media-Skills — a library of skills that let agents like , , and other coding assistants drive 200+ image/video models end-to-end (prompt → generate → edit → stitch) directly from your terminal. Perfect for building automated media pipelines without touching a UI.

🌐 Try it Online — No Install Required

Use all four studios (Image, Video, Lip Sync, Cinema) directly in your browser — no Node.js, no setup. Sign up for a free account to start generating. The hosted version is always up to date with the latest models.

Join Discord for discussions and support

Happy Horse top video model coming soon: Follow Happy Horse AI for updates

One-click installers — no Node.js or terminal required.

Because the app is not notarized by Apple, macOS Gatekeeper will block it on first launch. Follow these steps:

— Mount the DMG and drag the app to

— Open Terminal and run:

xattr -cr "/Applications/Open Generative AI.app"

— Right-click the app in → click → click again on the dialog

You only need to do this once. After that, the app opens normally.

Alternative (no Terminal):

  1. Try to open the app — macOS will block it
  2. Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security
  3. Scroll down to find "Open Generative AI was blocked"

Windows Installation — SmartScreen warning fix

Windows SmartScreen may show a warning because the installer is not code-signed:

  1. Click on the SmartScreen dialog

The app will install silently to with a Start Menu shortcut.

Ubuntu / Linux Installation

Linux artifacts are available when building with Electron Builder:

# Build Linux installers (AppImage + .deb)
npm run electron:build:linux

Generated files are written to the folder:

  • — portable, run directly after making executable:
    chmod +x "release/Open Generative AI-*.AppImage"
./release/Open\ Generative\ AI-*.AppImage
  • — install on Debian/Ubuntu:
    sudo apt install ./release/open-generative-ai_*_amd64.deb

If AppImage fails to start on older systems, install :

sudo apt install libfuse2

Ubuntu 24.04+ / AppArmor sandbox restriction

Ubuntu 24.04 and later enable a kernel security policy (apparmor_restrict_unprivileged_userns) that blocks Chromium's user-namespace sandbox. If the app fails to start silently or crashes immediately, you have two options:

Option A — Recommended: install the instead. The package ships an AppArmor profile that grants the required permission automatically on install with no system-wide changes.

Option B — Temporary system fix (AppImage users):

sudo sysctl -w kernel.apparmor_restrict_unprivileged_userns=0

This lasts until next reboot. To make it permanent:

echo 'kernel.apparmor_restrict_unprivileged_userns=0' | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.d/99-userns.conf

Open Generative AI is a free, uncensored, open-source AI image, video, cinema, and lip sync studio that brings unrestricted creative workflows to everyone. No content filters, no prompt rejections, no guardrails — just full creative freedom. Powered by Muapi.ai, it supports text-to-image, image-to-image, text-to-video, image-to-video, and audio-driven lip sync generation across models like Flux, Nano Banana, Midjourney, Kling, Sora, Veo, Seedream, Infinite Talk, LTX Lipsync, Wan 2.2, and more — all from a sleek, modern interface you can self-host and customize.

Why Open Generative AI instead of Higgsfield AI, Freepik, Krea AI, Openart AI?

  • Uncensored & unrestricted — no content filters, no nanny guardrails, no prompt rejections
  • — no subscription, no vendor lock-in
  • — your data stays on your machine, full creative control
  • — text-to-image, image-to-image, text-to-video, image-to-video, lip sync
  • — feed up to 14 reference images into compatible models
  • — animate portraits or sync lips to any audio with 9 dedicated models
  • — add your own models, modify the UI, build on top of it

⚡ Local Model Inference (Desktop App Only)

The desktop app includes a built-in powered by stable-diffusion.cpp — generate images entirely on your own machine with no API key and no internet connection required.

20-step anime/illustration

require two shared auxiliary files (downloaded once, shared across both models):

  • — 2.4 GB
  1. Open in the desktop app
  2. Install the (one click — auto-downloaded)
  3. Download your chosen model (and auxiliary files for Z-Image)
  4. In , click the toggle next to the model selector
  5. Select your local model and generate — no API key needed

All downloads happen inside the app. Nothing is installed system-wide.

Local inference is only available in the desktop app. The hosted web version always uses cloud APIs.

  • Runs on CPU (all platforms) and (macOS Apple Silicon — M1/M2/M3/M4)
  • Metal GPU acceleration is built into the macOS desktop binary — significantly faster than CPU-only
  • Recommended: 16 GB RAM for Z-Image models (7.4 GB weights + 2.4 GB compute buffer)
  • The system may slow during generation — the process uses all available CPU cores while running
  • — Generate images from text prompts (50+ text-to-image models) or transform existing images (55+ image-to-image models). Switches model set automatically based on whether a reference image is provided. Quality and resolution controls visible for models that support them.
  • — Generate images on-device with no API key using Z-Image Turbo/Base, Dreamshaper, Realistic Vision, Anything v5, or SDXL — powered by stable-diffusion.cpp with Metal GPU acceleration on Apple Silicon.
  • — Upload up to 14 reference images for compatible edit models (Nano Banana 2 Edit, Flux Kontext Dev, GPT-4o Edit, and more). Multi-select picker with order badges, batch upload, and a "Use Selected" confirmation flow.
  • — Generate videos from text prompts (40+ text-to-video models) or animate a start-frame image (60+ image-to-video models). Same intelligent mode switching as Image Studio.
  • — Animate portrait images or sync lips on existing videos using audio. 9 dedicated models across two modes: portrait image + audio → talking video, and video + audio → lipsync video.
  • — Interface for photorealistic cinematic shots with pro camera controls (Lens, Focal Length, Aperture)
  • — Build and run multi-step AI pipelines visually. Chain image, video, and audio models into automated flows. Browse community templates, create your own with a node-based editor, and run them via an interactive playground.
  • — Reference images are uploaded once and stored locally. A picker panel lets you reuse any previously uploaded image across sessions — no re-uploading.
  • — Dynamic aspect ratio, resolution/quality, and duration pickers that adapt to each model's capabilities (including t2i models with resolution or quality options)
  • — Browse, revisit, and download all past generations (persisted in browser storage)
  • — One-click download of generated outputs in full resolution
  • — Secure API key storage in browser localStorage (never sent to any server except Muapi)
  • — Works seamlessly on desktop and mobile with dark glassmorphism UI

🖼️ Image Studio — Dual Mode

The Image Studio automatically switches between two model sets:

50+ t2i models (Flux, Nano Banana 2, Seedream 5.0, Ideogram, GPT-4o, Midjourney…)
55+ i2i models (Kontext, Nano Banana 2 Edit, Seedream 5.0 Edit, Seededit, Upscaler…)
Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image · Resolution 1K/2K/4K · Google Search enhancement · aspect ratio
Up to · Resolution 1K/2K/4K · Google Search enhancement
ByteDance · Quality basic/high · 8 aspect ratios · up to 4K
ByteDance · Natural language style transfer · Quality basic/high
MiniMax · 8 aspect ratios · up to 4 images per request · 1500 char prompt

Models that accept multiple reference images expose a multi-select picker when active:

GPT-4o Edit / GPT Image 1.5 Edit
Bytedance Seedream Edit v4 / v4.5
Vidu Q2 Reference to Image
Qwen Image Edit Plus / 2511

When a multi-image model is selected the upload trigger switches to multi-select mode:

  • Checkboxes with order numbers — images are sent to the model in the order you select them
  • — pick multiple files at once from your file dialog
  • on the trigger shows how many images are active; a badge appears when more slots are available
  • confirms and closes the picker

🎬 Video Studio — Dual Mode

The Video Studio follows the same pattern:

40+ t2v models (Kling, Sora, Veo, Wan, Seedance 2.0, Hailuo, Runway…)
60+ i2v models (Kling I2V, Veo3 I2V, Runway I2V, Wan I2V, Seedance 2.0 I2V, Midjourney I2V…)
ByteDance · Aspect ratios 16:9 / 9:16 / 4:3 / 3:4 · Duration 5 / 10 / 15s · Quality basic/high
ByteDance · Animate images into video · Up to 9 reference images · Aspect ratios 16:9 / 9:16 / 4:3 / 3:4 · Duration 5 / 10 / 15s · Quality basic/high
ByteDance · Seamlessly continue any Seedance 2.0 generation · Preserves style, motion & audio · Optional continuation prompt · Duration 5 / 10 / 15s · Quality basic/high
xAI · Duration 6 / 10 / · Modes: fun / normal / spicy · Aspect ratios 9:16 / 16:9 / 2:3 / 3:2 / 1:1
xAI · Duration 6 / 10 / · Modes: fun / normal / spicy · Cinematic motion from still images
MiniMax Hailuo 02 / 2.3 Standard & ProText-to-Video / Image-to-VideoMiniMax · Full HD video · Multiple aspect ratios · Fast variant included

The generates audio-driven talking videos using 9 models across two input modes:

Upload a portrait image + audio file → animated talking video
Upload an existing video + audio file → lipsync video

Image-based Models (Portrait Image + Audio → Video)

infinitetalk-image-to-video

Video-based Models (Video + Audio → Lipsync Video)

infinitetalk-video-to-video
  1. Select or mode using the toggle
  2. Upload your portrait image (or video) using the image/video upload button
  3. Upload your audio file using the audio upload button
  4. Optionally enter a prompt to guide the motion style
  5. Select a model and resolution (where supported), then click

Generation history is saved separately in and pending jobs resume automatically on page reload.

The lets you build and run multi-step AI pipelines without writing code.

  • — Start from pre-built workflows (image chains, video pipelines, and more)
  • — Save and manage your own custom pipelines
  • — Browse and run workflows published by other users
  • — Drag-and-drop visual editor to connect models and route outputs between steps
  • — Run any workflow interactively with a form UI; results render inline
  • — Every workflow is also callable via the Muapi API

💡 Want to add workflows to your own app? Check out — the open-source workflow engine powering this feature. Drop it into any project.

The offers precise control over the virtual camera, translating your choices into optimized prompt modifiers:

Modular 8K Digital, Full-Frame Cine Digital, Grand Format 70mm Film, Studio Digital S35, Classic 16mm Film, Premium Large Format Digital
Creative Tilt, Compact Anamorphic, Extreme Macro, 70s Cinema Prime, Classic Anamorphic, Premium Modern Prime, Warm Cinema Prime, Swirl Bokeh Portrait, Vintage Prime, Halation Diffusion, Clinical Sharp Prime
8mm (Ultra-Wide), 14mm, 24mm, 35mm (Human Eye), 50mm (Portrait), 85mm (Tight Portrait)
f/1.4 (Shallow DoF), f/4 (Balanced), f/11 (Deep Focus)

📁 Upload History & Picker

Every image you upload is saved locally (URL + thumbnail) so you never upload the same file twice:

  • Click the upload button to open the
  • Previously uploaded images appear in a 3-column grid with thumbnails
  • — click a thumbnail to instantly select and close
  • — toggle multiple thumbnails (shown with order numbers), then click
  • Upload new images with the button (supports multi-file selection in multi-image mode)
  • Remove individual images from history with the ✕ button
  • History persists across browser sessions (stored in )
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/Anil-matcha/Open-Generative-AI.git
cd Open-Generative-AI

# Install dependencies (installs root + packages/studio workspace)
npm install

# Start the development server
npm run dev

Open in your browser. You'll be prompted to enter your Muapi API key on first use.

npm run build
npm run start

Build native desktop apps with Electron:

# macOS (DMG — Intel + Apple Silicon)
npm run electron:build

# Windows (NSIS installer — x64 + ARM64)
npm run electron:build:win

# Linux (AppImage + DEB — x64)
npm run electron:build:linux

# Both platforms in one pass
npm run electron:build:all

Installers are output to the folder. Pre-built binaries are also available on the Releases page.

The app is a with a shared component library.

Open-Generative-AI/
├── app/                        # Next.js App Router
│   ├── layout.js               # Root layout (Tailwind, fonts)
│   ├── page.js                 # Redirects → /studio
│   └── studio/
│       └── page.js             # Studio page — renders StandaloneShell
├── components/
│   ├── StandaloneShell.js      # Tab nav + BYOK (API key from localStorage)
│   └── ApiKeyModal.js          # API key entry modal
├── packages/
│   └── studio/                 # Shared React component library
│       └── src/
│           ├── index.js        # Exports: ImageStudio, VideoStudio, LipSyncStudio, CinemaStudio, WorkflowStudio
│           ├── models.js       # 200+ model definitions (single source of truth)
│           ├── muapi.js        # API client (named exports, apiKey as first param)
│           └── components/
│               ├── ImageStudio.jsx    # Dual-mode t2i/i2i studio
│               ├── VideoStudio.jsx    # Dual-mode t2v/i2v studio
│               ├── LipSyncStudio.jsx  # Portrait/video + audio → talking video
│               ├── CinemaStudio.jsx   # Pro studio with camera controls
│               └── WorkflowStudio.jsx # Multi-step pipeline builder & playground
├── next.config.mjs             # transpilePackages: ['studio']
├── tailwind.config.js
└── package.json                # workspaces: ["packages/studio"]

The library is also consumed by the hosted version on muapi.ai — model updates made in packages/studio/src/models.js apply to both the self-hosted app and the hosted version automatically.

The app communicates with Muapi.ai using a two-step pattern:

  1. — POST /api/v1/{model-endpoint} with prompt and parameters
  2. — GET /api/v1/predictions/{request_id}/result until status is

Authentication uses the header. During development, a Vite proxy handles CORS by routing requests to .

File uploads use (multipart/form-data) and return a hosted URL that is passed to image-conditioned models. For multi-image models the full array is forwarded to the API in one request.

Lip sync jobs use the same two-step pattern: a dedicated method accepts or alongside , dispatches to the model's endpoint, and polls until the output video URL is available.

🎨 Supported Model Categories

Flux Dev, Nano Banana 2, Seedream 5.0, Ideogram v3, Midjourney v7, GPT-4o, SDXL
Nano Banana 2 Edit (×14), Flux Kontext Pro, GPT-4o Edit, Seededit v3, Upscaler, Background Remover
Kling v3, Sora 2, Veo 3, Wan 2.6, Seedance 2.0, Seedance 2.0 Extend, Seedance Pro, Hailuo 2.3, Runway Gen-3
Kling v2.1 I2V, Veo3 I2V, Runway I2V, Seedance 2.0 I2V, Midjourney v7 I2V, Hunyuan I2V, Wan2.2 I2V
Infinite Talk I2V, Wan 2.2 Speech to Video, LTX 2.3 Lipsync, LTX 2 19B Lipsync, Sync, LatentSync, Creatify, Veed, Infinite Talk V2V
  • — App Router, server components, fast dev server
  • — Studio UI components
  • — Utility-first styling
  • — Monorepo with shared library

🤔 How is this different from Higgsfield AI, Freepik, Krea, Openart AI?

is a community-driven, open-source alternative that provides similar creative capabilities without the closed ecosystem:

Yes — prompts blocked or altered
Platform guardrails enforcedUnrestricted creative freedom
200+ open & commercial models
Up to 14 images per request
9 models, image & video modes

Built with Muapi.ai — the unified API for AI image and video generation models.

: For more details on the "AI Influencer" engine, upcoming "Popcorn" storyboarding features, and the future of this project, read the full technical overview.

Looking for a free, uncensored Higgsfield AI, Freepik, Krea, Openart AI alternative? Open Generative AI is an open-source, unrestricted AI image and video generation studio — a Higgsfield AI, Freepik, Krea, Openart AI replacement with no content filters that you can self-host, customize, and extend.

This project is an independent, experimental, and open-source initiative and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or associated with Higgsfield Inc., Freepik, Krea AI, OpenArt AI, or any of their respective companies, products, or services. Any references to third-party platforms, models, or technologies are made solely for interoperability, benchmarking, research, or educational purposes. All trademarks, logos, and brand names are the property of their respective owners. If any content in this repository creates confusion or raises concerns, please contact us and we will promptly review and address it.

items.title: huggingface/ml-intern
items.content:
🤗 ml-intern: an open-source ML engineer that reads papers, trains models, and ships ML models

An ML intern that autonomously researches, writes, and ships good quality ML releated code using the Hugging Face ecosystem — with deep access to docs, papers, datasets, and cloud compute.

git clone git@github.com:huggingface/ml-intern.git
cd ml-intern
uv sync
uv tool install -e .

That's it. Now works from any directory:

Create a file in the project root (or export these in your shell):

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=<your-anthropic-api-key> # if using anthropic models
HF_TOKEN=<your-hugging-face-token>
GITHUB_TOKEN=<github-personal-access-token>

If no is set, the CLI will prompt you to paste one on first launch. To get a GITHUB_TOKEN follow the tutorial here.

(start a chat session):

(single prompt, auto-approve):

ml-intern "fine-tune llama on my dataset"

ml-intern --model anthropic/claude-opus-4-6 "your prompt"
ml-intern --max-iterations 100 "your prompt"
ml-intern --no-stream "your prompt"

The agent emits the following events via :

  • - Starting to process user input
  • - Agent is ready for input
  • - Streaming token chunk
  • - Complete LLM response text
  • - Token stream finished
  • - Tool being called with arguments
  • - Tool execution result
  • - Informational tool log message
  • - Tool execution state transition
  • - Requesting user approval for sensitive operations
  • - Agent finished processing
  • - Error occurred during processing
  • - Agent was interrupted
  • - Context was compacted
  • - Undo operation completed
  • - Agent shutting down

                    "param": {"type": "string", "description": "Parameter description"}
                },
                "required": ["param"]
            },
            handler=your_async_handler
        ),
        # ... existing tools
    ]

{

}

items.title: vercel-labs/skills
items.content:
The open agent skills tool - npx skills

The CLI for the open agent skills ecosystem.

Supports , , , , and 41 more.

npx skills add vercel-labs/agent-skills

# GitHub shorthand (owner/repo)
npx skills add vercel-labs/agent-skills

# Full GitHub URL
npx skills add https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-skills

# Direct path to a skill in a repo
npx skills add https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-skills/tree/main/skills/web-design-guidelines

# GitLab URL
npx skills add https://gitlab.com/org/repo

# Any git URL
npx skills add git@github.com:vercel-labs/agent-skills.git

# Local path
npx skills add ./my-local-skills
Install to user directory instead of project
Install specific skills by name (use for all skills)
List available skills without installing
Copy files instead of symlinking to agent directories
Skip all confirmation prompts

# List skills in a repository
npx skills add vercel-labs/agent-skills --list

# Install specific skills
npx skills add vercel-labs/agent-skills --skill frontend-design --skill skill-creator

# Install a skill with spaces in the name (must be quoted)
npx skills add owner/repo --skill "Convex Best Practices"

# Install to specific agents
npx skills add vercel-labs/agent-skills -a claude-code -a opencode

# Non-interactive installation (CI/CD friendly)
npx skills add vercel-labs/agent-skills --skill frontend-design -g -a claude-code -y

# Install all skills from a repo to all agents
npx skills add vercel-labs/agent-skills --all

# Install all skills to specific agents
npx skills add vercel-labs/agent-skills --skill '*' -a claude-code

# Install specific skills to all agents
npx skills add vercel-labs/agent-skills --agent '*' --skill frontend-design
Committed with your project, shared with team
Available across all projects

When installing interactively, you can choose:

Creates symlinks from each agent to a canonical copy. Single source of truth, easy updates.
Creates independent copies for each agent. Use when symlinks aren't supported.

Search for skills interactively or by keyword
npx skills remove [skills]Remove installed skills from agents
npx skills update [skills]Update installed skills to latest versions

List all installed skills. Similar to .

Search for skills interactively or by keyword.

# Interactive search (fzf-style)
npx skills find

# Search by keyword
npx skills find typescript

# Update all skills (interactive scope prompt)

npx skills update my-skill

npx skills update frontend-design web-design-guidelines

npx skills update -p

Only update global skills
Only update project skills
Skip scope prompt (auto-detect: project if in a project dir, else global)
Update specific skills by name instead of all
# Create SKILL.md in current directory
npx skills init

# Create a new skill in a subdirectory
npx skills init my-skill

Remove installed skills from agents.

# Remove interactively (select from installed skills)
npx skills remove

# Remove specific skill by name
npx skills remove web-design-guidelines

# Remove multiple skills
npx skills remove frontend-design web-design-guidelines

# Remove from global scope
npx skills remove --global web-design-guidelines

# Remove from specific agents only
npx skills remove --agent claude-code cursor my-skill

# Remove all installed skills without confirmation

# Remove all skills from a specific agent

# Use 'rm' alias
npx skills rm my-skill
Remove from global scope (~/) instead of project
Remove from specific agents (use for all)
Specify skills to remove (use for all)
Skip confirmation prompts
Shorthand for --skill '*' --agent '*' -y

Agent skills are reusable instruction sets that extend your coding agent's capabilities. They're defined in files with YAML frontmatter containing a and .

Skills let agents perform specialized tasks like:

  • Generating release notes from git history
  • Creating PRs following your team's conventions
  • Integrating with external tools (Linear, Notion, etc.)

Skills can be installed to any of these agents:

Amp, Kimi Code CLI, Replit, Universal, , ,
~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/
~/.snowflake/cortex/skills/
~/.deepagents/agent/skills/
~/.config/opencode/skills/
~/.codeium/windsurf/skills/

After installing skills, manually add them to your custom agent's in .kiro/agents/<agent>.json:

{
  "resources": ["skill://.kiro/skills/**/SKILL.md"]
}

The CLI automatically detects which coding agents you have installed. If none are detected, you'll be prompted to select which agents to install to.

Skills are directories containing a file with YAML frontmatter:

---
name: my-skill
description: What this skill does and when to use it
---

# My Skill

Instructions for the agent to follow when this skill is activated.

## When to Use

Describe the scenarios where this skill should be used.

## Steps

1. First, do this
2. Then, do that
  • : Unique identifier (lowercase, hyphens allowed)
  • : Brief explanation of what the skill does
  • : Set to to hide the skill from normal discovery. Internal skills are only visible and installable when INSTALL_INTERNAL_SKILLS=1 is set. Useful for work-in-progress skills or skills meant only for internal tooling.
---
name: my-internal-skill
description: An internal skill not shown by default
metadata:
  internal: true
---

The CLI searches for skills in these locations within a repository:

  • Root directory (if it contains )

Plugin Manifest Discovery

If .claude-plugin/marketplace.json or .claude-plugin/plugin.json exists, skills declared in those files are also discovered:

// .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
{
  "metadata": { "pluginRoot": "./plugins" },
  "plugins": [
    {
      "name": "my-plugin",
      "source": "my-plugin",
      "skills": ["./skills/review", "./skills/test"]
    }
  ]
}

If no skills are found in standard locations, a recursive search is performed.

Skills are generally compatible across agents since they follow a shared Agent Skills specification. However, some features may be agent-specific:

Ensure the repository contains valid files with both and in the frontmatter.

Skill not loading in agent

  • Verify the skill was installed to the correct path
  • Check the agent's documentation for skill loading requirements
  • Ensure the frontmatter is valid YAML

Ensure you have write access to the target directory.

Set to or to show and install skills marked as
Set to disable anonymous usage telemetry
Alternative way to disable telemetry
# Install internal skills
INSTALL_INTERNAL_SKILLS=1 npx skills add vercel-labs/agent-skills --list

This CLI collects anonymous usage data to help improve the tool. No personal information is collected.

Telemetry is automatically disabled in CI environments.

items.title: AIDC-AI/Pixelle-Video
items.content:
🚀 AI 全自动短视频引擎 | AI Fully Automated Short Video Engine

只需输入一个 ，Pixelle-Video 就能自动完成：

    • ✅ : 新增「动作迁移」模块，上传参考视频和图片进行动作迁移
    • ✅ : 新增「数字人口播」和「图生视频」流水线，新增多语言 TTS 音色支持
    • ✅ : 新增 RunningHub 48G 显存机器调用支持
    • ✅ : 支持 RunningHub 并发限制可配置，优化 LLM 返回结构化数据的逻辑
    • ✅ : 支持 ComfyUI API Key 配置，支持 Nano Banana 模型调用，API 接口支持模板自定义参数
    • ✅ : 侧边栏内置 FAQ，锁定 edge-tts 版本修复 TTS 服务不稳定问题
    • ✅ : 支持固定脚本多种分割方式(段落/行/句子)，优化模板选择交互逻辑支持直接预览选择
    • ✅ : 修复视频生成 API 返回 URL 路径处理，支持跨平台兼容
    • ✅ : 新增 Windows 整合包下载，优化图片与视频反推工作流
    • ✅ : 新增「自定义素材」功能，支持用户上传自己的照片和视频，AI 智能分析生成脚本
    • ✅ : 优化 RunningHub 服务调用支持并行处理，新增历史记录页面，支持批量创建视频任务
    • ✅ - 根据主题智能创作解说词，无需自己写脚本
    • ✅ - 每句话都配上精美的 AI 插图
    • ✅ - 支持使用 AI 视频生成模型（如 WAN 2.1）创建动态视频内容
    • ✅ - 支持 Edge-TTS、Index-TTS 等众多主流 TTS 方案
    • ✅ - 支持 GPT、通义千问、DeepSeek、Ollama 等
    • ✅ - 基于 ComfyUI 架构，可使用预置工作流，也可自定义任意能力（如替换生图模型为 FLUX、替换 TTS 为 ChatTTS 等）

    Pixelle-Video 采用模块化设计，整个视频生成流程清晰简洁：

    从输入文本到最终视频输出，整个流程简洁清晰：文案生成 → 配图规划 → 逐帧处理 → 视频合成

    每个环节都支持灵活定制，可选择不同的 AI 模型、音频引擎、视觉风格等，满足个性化创作需求。

    以下是使用 Pixelle-Video 生成的实际案例，展示了不同主题和风格的视频效果：

    💡 : 这些视频都是通过输入一个主题关键词，由 AI 全自动生成的，无需任何视频剪辑经验！

    🪟 Windows 一键整合包（推荐 Windows 用户使用）

    无需安装 Python、uv 或 ffmpeg，一键开箱即用！

    1. 在「⚙️ 系统配置」中配置 LLM API 和图像生成服务

    💡 : 整合包已包含所有依赖，无需手动安装任何环境。首次使用只需配置 API 密钥即可。

    从源码安装（适合 macOS / Linux 用户或需要自定义的用户）

    在开始之前，需要先安装 Python 包管理器 和视频处理工具 ：

    安装完成后，在终端中运行 验证安装成功。

    sudo apt update
sudo apt install ffmpeg

    安装完成后，在终端中运行 验证安装成功。

    git clone https://github.com/AIDC-AI/Pixelle-Video.git
cd Pixelle-Video

    # 使用 uv 运行（推荐，会自动安装依赖）
uv run streamlit run web/app.py
    • : 选择 AI 模型（如通义千问、GPT 等）并填入 API Key
    • : 如需生成图片，配置 ComfyUI 地址或 RunningHub API Key

    打开 Web 界面后，你会看到三栏布局，下面详细讲解每个部分：

    首次使用时需要配置，点击展开「⚙️ 系统配置」面板：

    • 通过下拉菜单选择预设模型（通义千问、GPT-4o、DeepSeek 等）
    • 选择后会自动填充 base_url 和 model
    • 点击「🔑 获取 API Key」链接去注册并获取密钥
      • RunningHub API Key: 云端图像生成服务的密钥
      • : 输入主题，AI 自动创作文案
        • : 直接输入完整文案，跳过 AI 创作
        • : 选择预置的背景音乐（如 default.mp3）
        • : 将你的音乐文件（MP3/WAV 等）放到 文件夹
        • 从下拉菜单选择 TTS 工作流（支持 Edge-TTS、Index-TTS 等）
        • 系统会自动扫描 文件夹中的 TTS 工作流
        • 如果懂 ComfyUI，可以自定义 TTS 工作流
        • 上传参考音频文件用于声音克隆（支持 MP3/WAV/FLAC 等格式）
        • 适用于支持声音克隆的 TTS 工作流（如 Index-TTS）
          • 支持本地部署（selfhost）和云端（RunningHub）工作流
          • 如果懂 ComfyUI，可以放自己的工作流到 文件夹
            • 例如：Minimalist black-and-white matchstick figure style illustration, clean lines, simple sketch style
            • : 静态模板（无需AI生成媒体，纯文字样式）
            • : 图片模板（使用AI生成的图片作为背景）
            • : 视频模板（使用AI生成的视频作为背景）
            • 从下拉菜单选择模板，按尺寸分组显示（竖屏/横屏/方形）
            • 如果懂 HTML，可以在 文件夹创建自己的模板
            • 会显示实时进度（生成文案 → 生成配图 → 合成语音 → 合成视频）

                A: 生成时长取决于视频分镜数量、网络状况和 AI 推理速度，通常几分钟内即可完成。

                1. 更换 TTS 工作流或上传参考音频（改变语音效果）
                • : LLM 使用 Ollama（本地运行）+ ComfyUI 本地部署 = 0 元
                • : LLM 使用通义千问（成本极低，性价比高）+ ComfyUI 本地部署
                • : LLM 使用 OpenAI + 图像使用 RunningHub（费用较高但无需本地环境）

                ：本地有显卡建议完全免费方案，否则推荐使用通义千问（性价比高）

                Pixelle-Video 的设计受到以下优秀开源项目的启发：

items.title: open-metadata/OpenMetadata
items.content:
OpenMetadata is a unified metadata platform for data discovery, data observability, and data governance powered by a central metadata repository, in-depth column level lineage, and seamless team collaboration.

Empower your Data Journey with OpenMetadata

OpenMetadata is a unified metadata platform for data discovery, data observability, and data governance powered by a central metadata repository, in-depth column-level lineage, and seamless team collaboration. It is one of the fastest-growing open-source projects with a vibrant community and adoption by a diverse set of companies in a variety of industry verticals. Based on Open Metadata Standards and APIs, supporting connectors to a wide range of data services, OpenMetadata enables end-to-end metadata management, giving you the freedom to unlock the value of your data assets.

Contents:

OpenMetadata Consists of Four Main Components:

  • : These are the core definitions and vocabulary for metadata based on common abstractions and types. They also allow for custom extensions and properties to suit different use cases and domains.
  • : This is the central repository for storing and managing the metadata graph, which connects data assets, users, and tool-generated metadata in a unified way.
  • : These are the interfaces for producing and consuming metadata, built on top of the metadata schemas. They enable seamless integration of user interfaces and tools, systems, and services with the metadata store.
  • : This is a pluggable framework for ingesting metadata from various sources and tools to the metadata store. It supports about 84+ connectors for data warehouses, databases, dashboard services, messaging services, pipeline services, and more.

Key Features of OpenMetadata

: Find and explore all your data assets in a single place using various strategies, such as keyword search, data associations, and advanced queries. You can search across tables, topics, dashboards, pipelines, and services.

: Communicate, converse, and cooperate with other users and teams on data assets. You can get event notifications, send alerts, add announcements, create tasks, and use conversation threads.

Data Quality and Profiler: Measure and monitor the quality with to build trust in your data. You can define and run data quality tests, group them into test suites, and view the results in an interactive dashboard. With powerful collaboration, make data quality a shared responsibility in your organization.

: Enforce data policies and standards across your organization. You can define data domains and data products, assign owners and stakeholders, and classify data assets using tags and terms. Use powerful automation features to auto-classify your data.

: Use reports and platform analytics to understand how your organization's data is doing. Data Insights provides a single-pane view of all the key metrics to reflect the state of your data best. Define the Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and set goals within OpenMetadata to work towards better documentation, ownership, and tiering. Alerts can be set against the KPIs to be received on a specified schedule.

: Track and visualize the origin and transformation of your data assets end-to-end. You can view column-level lineage, filter queries, and edit lineage manually using a no-code editor.

: Document your data assets and metadata entities using rich text, images, and links. You can also add comments and annotations and generate data dictionaries and data catalogs.

: Monitor the health and performance of your data assets and pipelines. You can view metrics such as data freshness, data volume, data quality, and data latency. You can also set up alerts and notifications for any anomalies or failures.

: Secure your data and metadata using various authentication and authorization mechanisms. You can integrate with different identity providers for single sign-on and define roles and policies for access control.

: Integrate with external applications and services using webhooks. You can register URLs to receive metadata event notifications and integrate with Slack, Microsoft Teams, and Google Chat.

: Ingest metadata from various sources and tools using connectors. OpenMetadata supports about 84+ connectors for data warehouses, databases, dashboard services, messaging services, pipeline services, and more.

Install and Run OpenMetadata

Documentation and Support

We're here to help and make OpenMetadata even better! Check out OpenMetadata documentation for a complete description of OpenMetadata's features. Join our Slack Community to get in touch with us if you want to chat, need help, or discuss new feature requirements.

We ❤️ all contributions, big and small! Check out our CONTRIBUTING guide to get started, and let us know how we can help.

Don't want to miss anything? Give the project a ⭐ 🚀

A HUGE THANK YOU to all our supporters!

items.title: langfuse/langfuse
items.content:
🪢 Open source LLM engineering platform: LLM Observability, metrics, evals, prompt management, playground, datasets. Integrates with OpenTelemetry, Langchain, OpenAI SDK, LiteLLM, and more. 🍊YC W23

Langfuse is an open source LLM engineering platform. It helps teams collaboratively develop, monitor, evaluate, and AI applications. Langfuse can be and is .

  • LLM Application Observability: Instrument your app and start ingesting traces to Langfuse, thereby tracking LLM calls and other relevant logic in your app such as retrieval, embedding, or agent actions. Inspect and debug complex logs and user sessions. Try the interactive demo to see this in action.

  • Prompt Management helps you centrally manage, version control, and collaboratively iterate on your prompts. Thanks to strong caching on server and client side, you can iterate on prompts without adding latency to your application.

  • Evaluations are key to the LLM application development workflow, and Langfuse adapts to your needs. It supports LLM-as-a-judge, user feedback collection, manual labeling, and custom evaluation pipelines via APIs/SDKs.

  • Datasets enable test sets and benchmarks for evaluating your LLM application. They support continuous improvement, pre-deployment testing, structured experiments, flexible evaluation, and seamless integration with frameworks like LangChain and LlamaIndex.

  • LLM Playground is a tool for testing and iterating on your prompts and model configurations, shortening the feedback loop and accelerating development. When you see a bad result in tracing, you can directly jump to the playground to iterate on it.

  • Comprehensive API: Langfuse is frequently used to power bespoke LLMOps workflows while using the building blocks provided by Langfuse via the API. OpenAPI spec, Postman collection, and typed SDKs for Python, JS/TS are available.

Managed deployment by the Langfuse team, generous free-tier, no credit card required.

Run Langfuse on your own infrastructure:

  • # Get a copy of the latest Langfuse repository
git clone https://github.com/langfuse/langfuse.git
cd langfuse

# Run the langfuse docker compose
docker compose up

  • VM: Run Langfuse on a single Virtual Machine using Docker Compose.

  • Kubernetes (Helm): Run Langfuse on a Kubernetes cluster using Helm. This is the preferred production deployment.

Manual instrumentation using the SDKs for full flexibility.
Automated instrumentation using drop-in replacement of OpenAI SDK.
Automated instrumentation by passing callback handler to Langchain application.
Automated instrumentation via LlamaIndex callback system.
Automated instrumentation via Haystack content tracing system.
Python, JS/TS (proxy only)Use any LLM as a drop in replacement for GPT. Use Azure, OpenAI, Cohere, Anthropic, Ollama, VLLM, Sagemaker, HuggingFace, Replicate (100+ LLMs).
TypeScript toolkit designed to help developers build AI-powered applications with React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, Node.js.
Open source framework for building AI agents and multi-agent systems.
Directly call the public API. OpenAPI spec available.

Packages integrated with Langfuse:

Library to get structured LLM outputs (JSON, Pydantic)
Framework that systematically optimizes language model prompts and weights
Python toolkit for building LLM applications.
Easily run open source LLMs on your own machine.
Open source LLM platform for building distributed agents.
JS/TS no-code builder for customized LLM flows.
Python-based UI for LangChain, designed with react-flow to provide an effortless way to experiment and prototype flows.
Open source LLM app development platform with no-code builder.
Self-hosted LLM Chat web ui supporting various LLM runners including self-hosted and local models.
Open source LLM testing platform.
Open source chatbot platform.
Open source voice AI platform.
Open source LLM platform for building distributed agents.
Open source Python library to build web interfaces like Chat UI.
Open source LLM platform for building distributed agents.
Multi agent framework for agent collaboration and tool use.

Instrument your app and start ingesting traces to Langfuse, thereby tracking LLM calls and other relevant logic in your app such as retrieval, embedding, or agent actions. Inspect and debug complex logs and user sessions.

2️⃣ Log your first LLM call

Not using OpenAI? Visit our documentation to learn how to log other models and frameworks.

pip install langfuse openai

LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY="sk-lf-..."
LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY="pk-lf-..."
LANGFUSE_BASE_URL="https://cloud.langfuse.com" # 🇪🇺 EU region
# LANGFUSE_BASE_URL="https://us.cloud.langfuse.com" # 🇺🇸 US region

from langfuse import observe
from langfuse.openai import openai # OpenAI integration

@observe()
def story():
    return openai.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o",
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is Langfuse?"}],
    ).choices[0].message.content

@observe()
def main():
    return story()

main()

3️⃣ See traces in Langfuse

See your language model calls and other application logic in Langfuse.

Finding an answer to your question:

  • Our documentation is the best place to start looking for answers. It is comprehensive, and we invest significant time into maintaining it. You can also suggest edits to the docs via GitHub.
  • Use "Ask AI" to get instant answers to your questions.
  • Ask any question in our public Q&A on GitHub Discussions. Please include as much detail as possible (e.g. code snippets, screenshots, background information) to help us understand your question.
  • For time-sensitive queries, ping us via the in-app chat widget.

Your contributions are welcome!

  • Vote on Ideas in GitHub Discussions.
  • Open a PR - see CONTRIBUTING.md for details on how to setup a development environment.

This repository is MIT licensed, except for the folders. See LICENSE and docs for more details.

❤️ Open Source Projects Using Langfuse

Top open-source Python projects that use Langfuse, ranked by stars (Source):

We take data security and privacy seriously. Please refer to our Security and Privacy page for more information.

By default, Langfuse automatically reports basic usage statistics of self-hosted instances to a centralized server (PostHog).

  1. Understand how Langfuse is used and improve the most relevant features.
  2. Track overall usage for internal and external (e.g. fundraising) reporting.

The telemetry does not include raw traces, prompts, observations, scores, or dataset contents. We document the exact fields that are collected, where they are sent, and the implementation reference in our telemetry docs.

For Langfuse OSS, you can opt out by setting .

  • ：适合具体的技术问题。提问时请提供完整信息（截图、错误日志等），有助于快速定位。
  • ：建议优先在相关文章下的留言区交流。若需后台提问，文章是最好的"敲门砖"，我在后台都能感受到这份心意哟 (´▽`ʃ♡ƪ)。
  • ：关注公众号，回复「」即可加入。无论你是 AI 小白还是硬核开发者，想求助技术问题还是分享折腾心得，这里都欢迎你。群里主打互助交流和灵感碰撞，入群请先看群公告；提问时描述清楚问题、附上截图，群友有空就会帮忙，大家的实战经验往往比我一个人更快更全面 🤝

： 本项目为开源分享，非商业产品。把作者当朋友而非客服，沟通效率会更高哦！

  • ：建议查看【历史更新】，明确具体的【功能内容】
  • ：在浏览器中打开报告可自动切换宽屏布局，关键词分组和独立展区均支持 Tab 快速切换，搜索框实时过滤新闻标题，邮件客户端仍显示原始窄屏布局，零回归
  • ：一键切换深色主题，自动记住偏好，适合夜间阅读
  • ：鼠标悬停新闻序号即可复制标题和链接，方便快速分享
  • ：整页截图和分段截图合并为下拉式导出按钮，截图时自动还原干净布局
  • ：支持 宽屏切换、 暗色模式、 搜索、 查看快捷键提示
  • ：让 AI 写好的内容一键推送到飞书、钉钉、Telegram、邮件等 9 个渠道，Markdown 自动适配各平台格式，不用操心格式差异
  • ：新增 工具，告诉 AI 每个渠道支持什么格式、有什么限制，生成的内容排版更好看
  • ：超长消息自动按各渠道字节限制拆分（飞书 30KB、钉钉 20KB 等），配置读取自 config.yaml
  • ：ntfy 不再因为默认地址被误报为"已配置"
  • ：批次处理函数直接复用 trendradar 核心模块，不重复造轮子

    默认监控 11 个主流平台，也可自行增加额外的平台

    支持 RSS/Atom 订阅源抓取，按关键词分组统计（与热榜格式一致）：

    • ：RSS 与热榜使用相同的关键词匹配和显示格式
    • ：直接在 中添加 RSS 源
    • ：自动过滤超过指定天数的旧文章，避免重复推送。支持全局默认天数和单源独立设置

    💡 RSS 使用与热榜相同的 进行关键词过滤

    提供基于 Web 的图形化配置界面，无需手动编辑 YAML 文件，通过表单即可完成所有配置项的修改与导出。

    • 不想看到重复新闻 → 用 （增量监控）
    • 想看完整榜单趋势 → 用 （当前榜单）
    按周一到周日逐日编排：为每天分配不同时间段、推送模式和 AI 分析策略。每个时段可独立设置筛选方式（关键词/AI）和关注方向，实现不同时间看不同类型新闻。内置 5 种预设（always_on / morning_evening / office_hours / night_owl / custom），也可自定义。支持工作日/周末差异化、跨午夜时段、per-period 去重、时段冲突检测（v6.0.0 + v6.5.0）
    通过 调整各区域（热榜、新增热点、RSS、独立展示区、AI 分析）的显示顺序；通过 控制各区域是否显示（v5.2.0）
    =按关键词分组，=按平台分组（v4.6.0 新增）

    设置个人关键词（如：AI、比亚迪、教育政策），只推送相关热点，过滤无关信息

    用自然语言描述你的兴趣，AI 自动分类新闻，替代传统关键词匹配

    • ：在 中用日常语言写下关注方向，无需学习关键词语法
    • ：AI 先从兴趣描述提取结构化标签，再对新闻按标签批量分类打分
    • ：通过 精确控制推送质量，只推送高相关度新闻
    • ：AI 筛选失败时自动回退到关键词匹配，确保推送不中断
    • ：兴趣变更时 AI 自动评估变化幅度，决定增量或全量重分类
    • ： 支持 （默认）和 两种模式，Timeline 可按时段覆盖
    • ：不同时间段可以使用不同的关键词文件或 AI 兴趣描述。例如早上用"科技词库"快速过滤，晚上换成"金融兴趣"做 AI 深度筛选
    # config.yaml 快速启用示例
filter:
  method: ai          # keyword（默认）| ai
ai_filter:
  min_score: 6         # 推送最低分数阈值（1-10）

    💡 AI 筛选与 AI 分析/翻译共享模型配置，只需配置一次

    实时追踪新闻热度变化，让你不仅知道"什么在热搜"，更了解"热点如何演变"

    • ：记录每条新闻从首次出现到最后出现的完整时间跨度
    • ：统计新闻在不同时间段的排名变化和出现频次
    • ：实时识别新出现的热点话题，用🆕标记第一时间提醒
    • ：区分一次性热点话题和持续发酵的深度新闻
    • ：同一新闻在不同平台的排名表现，看出媒体关注度差异

    不再被各个平台的算法牵着走，TrendRadar 会重新整理全网热搜

    支持(+ 微信推送方案)、、、、、、、、（可对接 Discord、IFTTT 等任意平台），消息直达手机和邮箱

    将推送内容翻译为任意语言，打破语言壁垒，无论是阅读国内热点还是通过 RSS 订阅海外资讯，都能以母语轻松获取

    • ：在 中设置 ai_translation.enabled: true 和目标语言即可
    • ：支持 English、Korean、Japanese、French 等任意语言
    • ：自动批量翻译，减少 API 调用次数，节省成本
    • ：通过 ai_translation_prompt.txt 自定义翻译风格和术语
    • ：与 AI 分析功能共用 配置段的模型设置
    # config.yaml 快速启用示例
ai_translation:
  enabled: true
  language: "English"  # 翻译目标语言

    💡 翻译功能与 AI 分析功能共享模型配置，只需配置一次 即可同时使用两个功能

    ：以下是一些 RSS 订阅源合集，可按需选用

    ⚠️ 部分海外媒体内容可能涉及敏感话题，AI 模型可能拒绝翻译，建议根据实际需求筛选订阅源

    在浏览器中打开推送的 HTML 报告，自动解锁增强体验（邮件客户端不受影响）：

    • ：桌面端自动切换 1200px 宽屏布局，充分利用屏幕空间
    • ：关键词分组和独立展区均支持 Tab 导航，告别长页面翻滚

    💡 所有增强功能基于渐进增强，邮件客户端仍显示原始 600px 布局，零回归

    • ：GitHub Actions 环境默认，支持 S3 兼容协议（R2/OSS/COS 等），数据存储在云端，不污染仓库
    • ：Docker/本地环境默认，数据完全可控
    • ：定时自动爬取 + 远程云存储（需签到续期）
    • ：支持多架构容器化运行，数据本地存储
    • ：Windows/Mac/Linux 直接运行

    使用 AI 大模型对推送内容进行深度分析，自动生成热点洞察报告

    • ：自动分析热点趋势、关键词热度、跨平台关联、潜在影响
    • ：基于 LiteLLM 统一接口，支持 100+ AI 提供商（DeepSeek、OpenAI、Gemini、Anthropic、本地 Ollama 等），还支持备用模型自动切换
    • ：AI 的分析范围可以和推送不同——推送只发新增消息（避免打扰），但 AI 可以分析当天全部新闻（看完整趋势）
    • ：可选仅原始内容、仅 AI 分析、或两者都推送
    • ：通过 config/ai_analysis_prompt.txt 自定义分析角度
    • ：指定平台的热榜完整展示，适合想看完整排名的用户
    • ：RSS 源内容可完整展示，不受关键词限制
    • ：可独立开启 AI 对完整热榜的趋势分析，无需在推送中展示

    基于 MCP (Model Context Protocol) 协议的 AI 对话分析系统，让你用自然语言深度挖掘新闻数据

      运行后根目录生成 ，即为完整的新闻报告页面。

      ：点击 创建仓库，可部署到 Cloudflare Pages 或 GitHub Pages 等静态托管平台。

      ：启用 GitHub Pages 可获得在线访问地址，进入仓库 Settings → Pages 即可开启。效果预览

      ⚠️ 原 GitHub Actions 自动存储功能已下线（该方案曾导致 GitHub 服务器负载过高，影响平台稳定性）。

      从"被算法推荐绑架"变成"主动获取自己想要的信息"

      投资者、自媒体人、企业公关、关心时事的普通用户

      股市投资监控、品牌舆情追踪、行业动态关注、生活资讯获取

      • ：比 GitHub Actions 更稳定，数据本地存储（无需配置云存储）
      • ：你需要阅读了解下方的基础配置流程，然后跳转到 Docker 教程进行部署。

      🅱️ 方案二：GitHub Actions 部署（本章节内容 ⬇️）

      • ：没有服务器的用户，利用 GitHub 免费资源
      • ：需配置云存储以获得完整体验，且需定期签到续期

      点击本仓库页面右上角的绿色 按钮 → 选择 "Create a new repository"。

      • 后续文档中提到的 "Fork" 均可理解为 "Use this template"

      2️⃣ 第二步：设置 GitHub Secrets

      在你 Fork 后的仓库中，进入 > > >

      • ：每添加一个配置项，点击一次"New repository secret"按钮，填写一对"Name"和"Secret"
      • ：出于安全考虑，保存后重新编辑时，只能看到 Name（名称），看不到 Secret（值）的内容
      • ：Secret 的 Name（名称）必须下方列出的名称（如 、 等），不能自己随意修改或创造新名称，否则系统无法识别
      • ：系统会向所有配置的平台发送通知
      • ：必须使用下方展开内容中列出的固定名称（如 ）
      • ：填写你从对应平台获取的实际内容（如 Webhook 地址、Token 等）
      • 完成第 1-2 步后，请立即测试！测试成功后再根据需要调整配置（第 4 步）
      • ：打开你 fork 的项目主页，点击顶部的 标签
      • ：直接访问 https://github.com/你的用户名/TrendRadar/actions
      • ❌ 作者的项目：https://github.com/sansan0/TrendRadar/actions
      • ✅ 你的项目：https://github.com/你的用户名/TrendRadar/actions
      1. 找到 (必须得是这个字)点进去，点击右侧的 按钮运行
      • 手动测试不要太频繁，避免触发 GitHub Actions 限制
      • 点击 Run workflow 后需要刷新浏览器页面才能看到新的运行记录

      默认配置已可正常使用，如需个性化调整，了解以下文件即可：

      主配置文件：推送模式、时间窗口、平台列表、热点权重等
      config/frequency_words.txt
      config/ai_analysis_prompt.txtAI 提示词模板：自定义 AI 分析师的角色和分析维度
      .github/workflows/crawler.yml

      ：引入「活跃度检测」机制，GitHub Actions 需定期签到以维持运行。

      • ：有效期为 ，倒计时结束后服务将自动挂起。
      • ：在 Actions 页面手动触发 "Check In" workflow，即可重置 7 天有效期。
      • ： → →
      • ：
        • 如果 7 天都忘了签到，或许这些资讯对你来说并非刚需。适时的暂停，能帮你从信息流中抽离，给大脑留出喘息的空间。
        • GitHub Actions 是宝贵的公共计算资源。引入签到机制旨在避免算力的无效空转，确保资源能分配给真正活跃且需要的用户。感谢你的理解与支持。
        • ：Actions 每次运行都是全新环境，不保存文件。如果不配置云存储，项目将运行在（无增量推送、无历史追踪）。

        这是 v5.0.0 的核心功能，让 AI 帮你总结和分析新闻，建议尝试。

        在 GitHub Secrets (或 / ) 中添加：

        • : 你的 API Key（支持 DeepSeek、OpenAI 等）
        • : 服务商名称（如 , ）

        就这样，无需复杂部署，下次推送时你就会看到智能分析报告了。

        恭喜！现在你可以开始享受 TrendRadar 带来的高效信息流了。

        💬 ：欢迎关注公众号「」，分享你的使用心得和高级玩法。

        TrendRadar 提供了两种 AI 使用方式，满足不同需求：

        👉 ：先用 满足日常需求；如果你是数据分析师或需要深度挖掘，再尝试 。

        ：本章节提供详细的配置说明，建议先完成 快速开始 的基础配置，再根据需要回来查看详细选项。

        在 文件中告诉机器人你想看什么，它就会帮你盯着。支持普通词、必须词、过滤词等多种玩法。

        TrendRadar 提供两个独立的 Docker 镜像，可根据需求选择部署：

        • 只需要推送功能：仅部署 镜像

        TrendRadar v3.0.0 新增了基于 MCP (Model Context Protocol) 的 AI 分析功能，让你可以通过自然语言与新闻数据对话，进行深度分析。

        AI 分析功能直接查询网络实时数据，而是分析你（存储在 文件夹中）

        1. ： 目录默认包含 一周的热榜新闻数据，可用于快速体验 AI 功能

          • ✅ 只能查询已有日期范围内的数据（12月21-27日，共7天）
          • 测试数据仅供快速体验，获取实时数据

        Cherry Studio 提供 GUI 配置界面，5 分钟快速部署，复杂的部分是一键安装的。

        ：现已更新到我的公众号，回复 "mcp" 即可

        • ：一次配置后续无需重复配置，中仅以此模式的配置为例。
        • ：如果 STDIO 模式配置遇到问题，可使用 HTTP 模式。此模式的配置方式与 STDIO 基本一致，但复制粘贴的内容就一行，不易出错。唯一需要注意的是每次使用前都需要手动启动一下服务。详细请参考 README-Cherry-Studio.md 底部的 HTTP 模式说明。

        💡 ：实际不建议一次性问多个问题。如果你选择的 AI 模型连下图的按顺序调用都无法做到，建议换一个。

        TrendRadar MCP 服务支持标准的 Model Context Protocol (MCP) 协议，可以接入各种支持 MCP 的 AI 客户端进行智能分析。

        • 将 替换为你的项目实际路径
        • Windows 路径使用双反斜杠：C:\\Users\\YourName\\TrendRadar
